examples.title: Function Template - Maximum of Two Values
examples.description: Generic function to find maximum of two values of any comparable type
examples.explanation: The template parameter T is replaced with the actual type at compile time. The same function body works for int, double, char, or any type that supports the > operator.

examples.title: Class Template - Generic Stack
examples.description: Implementation of a generic stack class template with basic operations
examples.explanation: This class template creates type-specific stacks. Stack<int> creates a stack for integers, Stack<string> for strings. The MAX parameter allows specifying stack capacity at compile time.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for push, pop, peek; O(n) for display

examples.title: Template Specialization - String Comparison
examples.description: Demonstrates full template specialization for handling char* differently from other types
examples.explanation: The primary template handles normal types using > operator. For char*, the specialized version uses strcmp() because > doesn't work properly for C-strings. The compiler picks the most specific matching template.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for primitive types; O(n) for string comparison

examples.title: Partial Class Template Specialization
examples.description: Demonstrates partial specialization for pointer types and const types
examples.explanation: Partial specialization allows creating more specific versions of the template. When Array<int*, 5> is used, the compiler picks the pointer specialization because it's more specific than the primary template.
